Coagulation

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The results indicate mild acidemia (low arterial and venous pH) with normal bicarbonate and mildly elevated venous pCO₂, suggesting a primary respiratory acidosis. Arterial oxygen saturation is slightly reduced. Coagulation (ACT) and magnesium are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation for underlying respiratory compromise and monitoring of oxygenation and acid-base status.
